name: Union-Find (Disjoint Set)
def: Tracks a partition of elements into disjoint sets with near-constant union and find.
problem: Maintain connected components / equivalence classes under incremental merging.
mechanism: A forest of parent pointers; find follows parents to a root, path compression flattens chains, and union-by-rank keeps trees shallow.
limits: Not ordered; no enumeration of a set cheaply
failures: Forgetting path compression / union-by-rank → O(n) chains
Assuming sets can be separated again
whenNot: Sets must be split, or membership enumerated
myths: "Union-find is O(1)" — it is inverse-Ackermann amortised, effectively but not exactly constant

x.invariant: Each element points toward a representative root; two elements share a set iff they reach the same root.
x.memoryOverhead: One parent index per element, plus a rank/size byte — extremely compact.
x.locality: Good after path compression flattens trees; early finds may chase pointers.
x.concurrency: Concurrent variants exist (lock-free union-find) but the basic structure is single-threaded.
implementations: Hand-rolled int array (ubiquitous in competitive/graph code)
